extreme.label: Extreme
extreme.summary: Volatile or high-resistance target. Expect narrow windows and punish mistakes.

advanced.label: Advanced
advanced.summary: Bring a tuned ship loadout. Solo mining is possible but less forgiving.

standard.label: Standard
standard.summary: Routine mining target. Starter and solo setups can work if the rock mass is reasonable.

C-Type.note: Community-observed C-Type returns often show around 1700, with higher multiples commonly indicating clusters.

E-Type.note: Community-observed E-Type returns are commonly reported around 1870-1900, with 5700 often treated as an E-Type cluster multiple.

I-Type.note: Ice-specific return reported by miners for I-Type rocks; treat as ice-only guidance.

M-Type.note: M-Type often appears around 1850, with 5550 treated as a likely cluster multiple.

P-Type.note: P-Type is commonly associated with 1750, with 5250 treated as a cluster multiple.

Q-Type.note: Q-Type is often tracked around 1870 in community mining references.

S-Type.note: S-Type is commonly reported around 1720 in community observations.

golem.label: Starter Golem
golem.summary: Entry-level ship miner for learning scan discipline, easier rocks, and short solo runs.
modules: 2 module slots on the Pitman head
Keep the stock starter fit until you learn your scan thresholds
scanWorkflow: Ping wide, then slow down and rescan once a contact resolves into a single ship-mining rock or small cluster.
Detailed scan every rock before you touch the laser. Walk away from high resistance, high instability, or ugly mass.
Prioritize easy cracks and volume. The Golem is strongest when you skip bad rocks quickly instead of forcing them.

conditional: E-Type on lighter rocks with manageable detailed-scan values

cannotMine: Do not plan the Golem around hard M/P rocks or big volatile rocks.
Skip ROC and hand-mining nodes entirely. This page is only for ship-mining targets.

prospector.label: Solo Prospector
prospector.summary: Best all-around solo mining ship for real throughput, tuned loadouts, and refinery-backed loops.
modules: Carry Surge and Stampede as situational swaps
scanWorkflow: Use broad ping sweeps first, then commit only after the detailed scan shows workable resistance, instability, and mass.
Prioritize stable solo cracks. If the rock only looks possible with perfect execution, skip it and keep moving.
For volatile ore, crack efficiently and transition to collection/refinery routing without wasting time on marginal rocks.

conditional: Quantanium-style volatile rocks when mass is manageable
avoid: oversized extreme rocks that clearly need crew support

cannotMine: A Prospector can technically touch more than it should. The hard stop is detailed-scan viability, not the resource name.
If the rock needs gadgets, perfect timing, and luck just to crack, that is usually MOLE work instead.

mole.label: Crew MOLE
mole.summary: Crew-optimized mining platform for hard rocks, mixed beam support, and heavy refinery runs.
heads: Lancet MH2 as support / extra power
modules: Carry Surge and Stampede for hard fractures
scanWorkflow: Use the pilot scan pass to find candidate clusters, then detailed scan before assigning turret roles.
Split responsibilities: one turret keeps the rock stable while the support beam adds controlled power.
The MOLE is where you deliberately take on rocks a solo ship should skip, especially volatile or high-instability targets.

conditional: Ice-specific I-Type rocks when they fit the crew objective and location

cannotMine: The MOLE does not replace FPS or ROC mining. It is only for ship-mining deposits.
Small easy rocks are still mineable, but using a MOLE there is an efficiency choice, not a capability requirement.

stock.label: No
stock.reason: Do not plan this resource with the ship in its stock state.

modules.label: No
modules.reason: Modules are not a reliable fix for this target on this ship.
modules.usage: Do not chase this with upgrades.

gadgets.label: No
gadgets.reason: Gadgets do not make this a clean choice for this ship.

stock.label: Yes
stock.reason: The stock Pitman setup is acceptable for this target if the detailed scan stays favorable.

modules.label: No
modules.reason: No mandatory module change is needed for this target on the Golem.
modules.usage: Fly stock and skip bad scans.

gadgets.label: Optional
gadgets.reason: Gadgets help only if the charge window feels tight.
gadgets.usage: Use Okunis when charge control feels rough. Use WaveShift when the optimal window is too small.

stock.label: Yes
stock.reason: The stock Arbor MH1 Prospector setup is workable for this target.

modules.label: No
modules.reason: You do not need upgrades to make this a valid target.
modules.usage: Upgrades improve margin, not baseline viability.

gadgets.label: Optional
gadgets.reason: Only bring gadgets if you want extra safety or cleaner control.
gadgets.usage: Use Okunis for easier control. Use WaveShift if the window feels narrow.

stock.label: No
stock.reason: Stock Prospector is not the right answer for this target.

modules.label: Yes
modules.reason: A tuned laser setup is the minimum upgrade path.
modules.usage: Swap to Helix I, fit Rieger-C3 and Focus III, then reassess with the detailed scan before starting the crack.

gadgets.label: Yes
gadgets.reason: Use gadgets when the upgraded fit still needs help stabilizing the crack.
gadgets.usage: Use WaveShift when the green zone is too small. Use BoreMax on unstable rocks. Use Okunis if charge control still feels rough.

stock.label: Yes
stock.reason: A stock MOLE can attempt this, but it is a crew job and not a casual solo target.

modules.label: Recommended
modules.reason: Upgrades make the crack materially safer and more controllable.
modules.usage: Fit these on the working head to improve control before you add more power.

gadgets.label: Yes
gadgets.reason: Gadgets are the normal mitigation path on the hardest rocks.
gadgets.usage: Place BoreMax on unstable rocks. Use WaveShift to widen the window. Use Okunis if the crew still struggles to hold the bar cleanly.

stock.label: Yes
stock.reason: The stock Arbor MH2 MOLE setup is valid for this target with crew discipline.

modules.label: Optional
modules.reason: Modules improve control and throughput but are not mandatory for baseline viability.
modules.usage: Fit these if you want a safer, more forgiving crack.

gadgets.label: Optional
gadgets.reason: Use gadgets only when the detailed scan shows the rock is trending unstable or tight.
gadgets.usage: Use BoreMax for instability. Use WaveShift for a narrow charge window.
